commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 307 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 307's diesel HDi and petrol TU/EW engines are generally robust when maintained, but turbo failures, timing belt neglect, and injector wear drive most replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific issue.

problems.h4: Peugeot 307 1.6 HDi Turbo Failure - Why DV6 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seals degrade over time, allowing oil into the intake or exhaust; carbon buildup on variable vanes causes sticking and loss of boost.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo seals, clean vanes, new oil feed pipe
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before shaft damage; 20-30k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new/reconditioned turbo, new gaskets, oil flush
repairOptions.longevity: Good solution if bottom end is healthy; 40-60k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DV6 engine with upgraded turbo, injectors tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when turbo debris has damaged bearings or pistons; like-new reliability

problems.recommendation: If your 307 shows early turbo symptoms (whistling, blue smoke, loss of power) below 70k miles, a specialist turbo rebuild may be cost-effective. However, if the engine has suffered oil starvation or metal debris has entered the sump, replacement with a rebuilt DV6 unit is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6TED4 engine prices for your Peugeot 307

problems.h4: Peugeot 307 Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Interference Damage
problems.rootCause: Timing belt snaps due to age, oil contamination, or tensioner failure; as an interference engine, valves collide with pistons causing severe internal damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump, seals
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance every 72k miles/5 years; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt TU/EW engine with new belt kit fitted, valves/pistons replaced, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once belt has snapped; rebuilt unit includes upgraded components

problems.recommendation: There is no middle ground with timing belt failure on the 307's petrol engines. If the belt has snapped, the engine is effectively written off. Invest in a rebuilt TU5JP4 or EW10 unit with a fresh belt kit already fitted - it's more reliable and often cheaper than attempting a head repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for NFU/RFJ rebuilt Peugeot 307 petrol engines

problems.h4: Peugeot 307 Injector Failure - HDi Rough Running & Misfire
problems.rootCause: Piezo or solenoid injectors wear internally, causing poor spray pattern, fuel leakage into cylinders, and uneven combustion; carbon buildup accelerates wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only one injector failed and engine is otherwise healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all injectors, new seals, fuel system clean, ECU coding
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if multiple injectors show wear; restores smooth running

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt HDi engine with tested/replaced injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if injector failure has caused cylinder wash-down or bearing wear

problems.recommendation: If your 307 HDi is misfiring but compression is good, replacing the faulty injector(s) at a specialist is cost-effective. However, if injector leakage has caused oil dilution or bearing damage, a rebuilt DV6/DW10 engine with pre-tested injectors is the smarter long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare HDi engine prices with tested injectors for Peugeot 307

problems.h4: Peugeot 307 Head Gasket Failure - Petrol Engine Overheating
problems.rootCause: Coolant leaks or thermostat failure cause overheating; aluminium cylinder head warps, breaking the head gasket seal and allowing coolant/oil mixing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, machine surface, new gasket, timing kit, coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged and head hasn't cracked; 30-50k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt TU engine with new head gasket, upgraded cooling components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if block is warped or head is cracked; like-new reliability

problems.recommendation: Head gasket repair can work on low-mileage 307 petrol engines if caught early. However, given the age of most 307s today, a rebuilt TU3JP or TU5JP4 engine with upgraded cooling components often represents better value and longer-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt TU petrol engines for your Peugeot 307

problems.h4: Peugeot 307 DPF Blockage - HDi Short-Journey Damage
problems.rootCause: Frequent short trips prevent DPF regeneration; soot accumulates, increasing backpressure, reducing power, and potentially damaging the turbo or EGR system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car DPF clean or forced regeneration via diagnostic tool
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if blockage is mild and no physical damage; temporary fix for short-journey use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new/reconditioned DPF, new sensors, ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Good solution if DPF is physically damaged; ensure driving habits change to prevent recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt HDi engine, DPF delete option available (off-road use), 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if DPF failure has caused turbo damage or excessive oil contamination

problems.recommendation: If your 307 HDi is used mainly for short trips, DPF issues are likely to recur. Consider a rebuilt DV6/DW10 engine with the option of DPF removal for off-road use, or commit to regular motorway runs to maintain regeneration cycles.
problems.cta: Compare HDi engine options with/without DPF for Peugeot 307

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 307
finalCta.paragraph: Whether it's turbo failure, timing belt damage, injector wear, head gasket issues, or DPF blockage, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 307 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road with confidence - without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 307 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 307 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot 307 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot 307 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Peugeot 307 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The smallest diesel option for the 307, the 1.4 HDi was offered in early Access and Active trims; economical but less common in the replacement market due to lower UK registration numbers.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging at higher mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 307 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 307 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the 307 diesel range; the 1.6 HDi (DV6 family) powered most UK sales from 2004 onwards, offering strong economy but known for turbo and injector wear.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 307 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 307 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The performance diesel choice; the 2.0 HDi (DW10 family) delivered strong torque for towing and motorway use, with later 136hp variants adding variable-geometry turbo complexity.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 307 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 307 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Entry-level petrol power; the 1.4 8v TU3JP offered simplicity and low running costs but head gasket failures on high-mileage units drive replacement demand.

items.title: Peugeot 307 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common 307 petrol engine; the 1.6 16v TU5JP4 balanced performance and economy but is an interference engine where timing belt neglect causes catastrophic damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt snap causing valve damage

items.title: Peugeot 307 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Performance petrol option; the 2.0 16v EW10 family powered the XSi, GTi and CC variants, offering strong acceleration but requiring diligent oil maintenance to avoid bearing wear. fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Peugeot 307 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.4 petrol to the high-output 2.0 HDi.

items.title: Peugeot 307 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel power dominated the UK 307 range from 2004 onwards, with 1.6 and 2.0 HDi units offering strong economy but known turbo, injector and DPF failure patterns.
items.descriptor: Diesel power dominated the UK 307 range from 2004 onwards, with 1.6 and 2.0 HDi units offering strong economy but known turbo, injector and DPF failure patterns.
families: DV6TED4 - 1.6 HDi 90/110hp (9HY/9HZ), most common replacement engine, turbo failure risk
DW10ATED - 2.0 HDi 110hp (RHY), robust bottom end but injector seat wear common
DW10BTED4 - 2.0 HDi 136hp (RHR), high-output with VGT turbo, actuator failures reported
foundIn: Facelift Hatchback & SW (2004-2009) - DV6 1.6 HDi, DW10 2.0 HDi
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy (50-60+ mpg)
Turbo oil seal failures between 60k-90k miles on 1.6 HDi
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey vehicles
High UK replacement demand on 1.6 HDi 110 variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 307 Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Peugeot 307 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol 307s used simple 8v and refined 16v TU/EW engines; reliable when maintained but timing belt neglect and head gasket failures drive replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Petrol 307s used simple 8v and refined 16v TU/EW engines; reliable when maintained but timing belt neglect and head gasket failures drive replacement enquiries.
families: TU3JP - 1.4 8v 75hp (KFV), entry-level, head gasket failures common on high mileage
TU5JP4 - 1.6 16v 110hp (NFU), most common petrol, interference engine - belt critical
EW10A/J4 - 2.0 16v 138-177hp (RFJ/RFR), performance units, oil maintenance essential
knownFor: Simple, affordable repairs on 1.4/1.6 8v units
Catastrophic valve damage if timing belt snaps (interference design)
Oil consumption on high-mileage 2.0 16v units
Strong enthusiast following for XSi/GTi performance variants

items.title: Peugeot 307 Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The Peugeot 307 was never offered with hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all variants use conventional petrol or diesel engines.
items.descriptor: The Peugeot 307 was never offered with hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all variants use conventional petrol or diesel engines.
items.familiesLabel: Common Peugeot 307 Hybrid / Electrified Engine Families
importantNotes: No factory hybrid, MHEV, PHEV or BEV variants were produced for the 307
All replacement enquiries relate to conventional combustion engines only
Aftermarket conversions exist but are not covered by EngineMarket's warranty terms

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 307 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 307's 2001-2009 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Generation: TU Petrol & Early HDi Diesel
years.description: The original 307 launched with simple TU petrol engines and the first-generation DW10 2.0 HDi diesel, establishing the model's core powertrain lineup.
keyChanges: Peugeot 307 launched replacing the 306; new platform with improved crash safety
No 1.6 HDi option yet; diesel buyers chose the larger 2.0 unit
knownFor: Simple, robust TU petrol engines when timing belts are maintained
Early DW10 diesels reliable but injector seat wear emerges at high mileage
Build quality praised but electrical gremlins reported on early examples
Strong residual values for well-maintained examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2003 Peugeot 307 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 307 2.0 petrol engine replacement • Peugeot 307 2.0 HDi injector failure • Peugeot 307 timing belt cost

years.description: The 2004 facelift introduced the game-changing 1.6 HDi DV6 engine, which would become the most common 307 diesel and the most frequently replaced unit.
keyChanges: Exterior refresh: new front bumper, lights, interior trim updates
Introduction of 1.6 HDi DV6TED4 engine (9HY/9HZ codes) replacing many 2.0 HDi applications
SW estate and CC convertible body styles added to range
DPF introduced on later 2.0 HDi 136 variants
knownFor: 1.6 HDi offers excellent economy but turbo failures emerge at 60k-90k miles
DV6 injector wear becomes a common high-mileage issue
GTi variant highly sought after by enthusiasts; EW10J4S engine robust when maintained
DPF-equipped models require motorway runs to prevent blockages
years.ticker: Peugeot 307 1.6 HDi turbo failure • Peugeot 307 DV6 injector replacement • Peugeot 307 GTi engine for sale

years.description: The final years saw DPF fitted across most HDi variants, tighter emissions compliance, and the 307's gradual replacement by the 308 in showrooms.
keyChanges: DPF became standard on most 1.6/2.0 HDi variants to meet Euro 4 emissions
Minor interior updates and equipment revisions
Production wound down as Peugeot 308 preparation began; final 307s built in 2009
Used market values stabilised as model became a budget-focused choice
mainEngines: 9HZ - 1.6L diesel (110hp) with DPF (most common late-model diesel)
RFJ - 2.0L petrol (140hp) (final petrol option for non-performance trims)
knownFor: DPF blockage becomes the dominant failure mode on short-journey HDi models
Late-model 307s offer strong value but engine replacement costs must be weighed against vehicle worth
SW estate variants remain popular for load-carrying; engine compatibility identical to hatchback
Petrol variants increasingly chosen by low-mileage urban drivers avoiding DPF complexity
years.ticker: Peugeot 307 DPF removal cost • Peugeot 307 2008 engine replacement • Peugeot 307 SW diesel engine

faq.h2: Peugeot 307 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 307 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Peugeot 307 engine typically costs £600-£1,900 supply-only, depending on fuel type and engine code. Supply & fit adds £400-£800 labour. Get personalised quotes to compare prices from UK specialists.
keyPoints: 1.4/1.6 petrol rebuilt: £600-£1,300 supply only
1.6/2.0 HDi diesel rebuilt: £800-£1,900 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£800
Used engines available from £400 but warranty terms vary
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 307 engine replacement quotes today

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Peugeot 307?
items.answer: Yes, if your 307 is in good condition otherwise. A rebuilt engine costing £800-£1,500 can extend life by 50k+ miles, often cheaper than buying a newer used car with unknown history.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine adds 50k+ reliable miles when maintained
Cheaper than purchasing a newer used replacement vehicle
Retain a car you know the history of
Factor in bodywork, interior condition and service history
items.cta: Get a personalised Peugeot 307 replacement value assessment

items.question: What is the most common Peugeot 307 engine problem?
items.answer: Turbo failure on 1.6 HDi (DV6) engines is the most frequent replacement driver. Timing belt neglect on petrol TU engines also causes catastrophic interference damage requiring full replacement.
keyPoints: 1.6 HDi turbo oil seals fail 60k-90k miles
Petrol TU engines are interference design - belt critical
Injector wear and DPF blockage common on high-mileage HDi
Head gasket failures on overheated petrol units
items.cta: Find Peugeot 307 engines with upgraded failure-point components

items.question: Which Peugeot 307 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 2.0 HDi 110hp (DW10ATED/RHY) is widely regarded as the most robust 307 engine when maintained. Avoid neglected 1.6 HDi units with unknown service history.
keyPoints: 2.0 HDi 110hp has robust bottom end and simpler turbo
Regular oil changes critical for all HDi longevity
Petrol TU engines reliable if timing belt replaced on schedule
Check service history before purchasing any used 307
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 HDi Peugeot 307 engine prices

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot 307 engine myself?
items.answer: Technically possible for experienced DIYers with proper tools, but not recommended. Engine replacement requires specialist equipment, ECU coding, and road testing best left to professionals.
keyPoints: Requires engine crane, torque wrenches, diagnostic tool
ECU may need adaptation/coding after engine swap
Risk of improper installation voiding warranty
Supply & fit service available nationwide for peace of mind
items.cta: Find Peugeot 307 engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 307 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines can be delivered next-day in many UK areas. Full supply & fit typically takes 2-4 working days depending on garage workload and engine availability.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery available on many rebuilt/used units
Garage fitting typically 1-2 days labour
Allow extra time for ECU adaptation and road testing
Book supply & fit in advance during peak periods
items.cta: Check live Peugeot 307 engine availability and delivery times

items.question: What engines fit a Peugeot 307?
items.answer: Only engines with matching engine codes fit your specific 307 variant. Use your registration to verify compatibility - common codes include DV6TED4, DW10ATED, TU5JP4 and EW10A.
keyPoints: Engine code must match your vehicle's original specification
DV6 fits 1.6 HDi variants; DW10 fits 2.0 HDi variants
TU5JP4 for 1.6 petrol; EW10A for 2.0 petrol applications
Cross-compatibility between 307, 308, C4, C5 limited - verify first
items.cta: Verify your Peugeot 307 engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: Do Peugeot 307 replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - all engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Terms vary by supplier; confirm details before ordering.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt units
Used engines typically 3-6 months; check supplier terms
Warranty covers parts and labour at supplying garage
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 307 engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Can I upgrade my Peugeot 307 engine to a more powerful one?
items.answer: Engine swaps are complex and may affect insurance, MOT and legality. Stick to direct replacement engines with matching codes unless pursuing a specialist project with full documentation.
keyPoints: Direct replacement requires matching engine code and ECU
Power upgrades may require ECU remap and supporting mods
Insurance must be notified of any engine modifications
Non-standard swaps risk MOT failure and warranty voidance
items.cta: Discuss Peugeot 307 engine upgrade options with a specialist

items.question: Where can I find the engine code on my Peugeot 307?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the gearbox bellhousing) and listed on your V5C registration document under "D.2 Engine number".
keyPoints: Check V5C document section D.2 for engine number/code
Physical stamp on engine block near gearbox flange
Service book or dealer records may also list the code
Enter your registration online for instant code verification
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 307 engine code in seconds
